tests.response_snapshot:
🌟 AItzik's TOP RECOMMENDATIONS

━━ TOYOTA YARIS ━━
💡 Why this model: Meets the user's minimum year requirement (2020-2025), and the listed used prices (starting around £10,000 up to ~£18,000+ for newer examples) are well under the user's £/ $50,000 cap. The Yaris is extremely fuel-efficient (official 68.9mpg; strong real-world economy around 60mpg and up to 80mpg in town), cheap to insure (groups 13–14) and inexpensive to service (£310 full service). Android Auto is standard and safety kit is strong (Euro NCAP five-star), making it a practical, low-running-cost Toyota from 2020 onward.

🌟 AItzik's TOP RECOMMENDATIONS

━━ MITSUBISHI OUTLANDER ━━
💡 Why this model: Post-2019 Outlander models fall within the user's minimum year requirement and are described as a spacious, family-friendly large SUV with a five-star Euro NCAP rating. The PHEV variant offers very low running costs for short-distance family use, and the petrol/all-wheel-drive variants tow well and provide practical boot space for family needs. Retrieved pricing indicates post-facelift 2019–2021 cars are priced at about £20,000–£22,000, which aligns with the user's upper budget limit as stated in the retrieved data. (Note: servicing costs are higher than average and the interior/ride/ refinement are described as less competitive versus rivals.)
